_comment: Provenance for every image used by the guided course. Kit-item photos are reusable across days (course-assets/assets/shared/); per-day diagrams are extracted from the official Freenove PDFs (course-assets/assets/day-NN/). Every entry records where it came from so attribution stays honest (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0). See docs/wayfinder/asset-workflow.md.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — ESP32-S3 development board photo
shared.alt: Official manual photo of the ESP32-S3 development board.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — GPIO extension board image
shared.alt: Official manual image of the ESP32-S3 GPIO extension board.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor image
shared.alt: Official manual image of the HC-SR04 ultrasonic distance sensor.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — jumper wire image
shared.alt: Official manual image of a jumper wire.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — Arduino IDE screenshot
shared.alt: Official manual screenshot of the Arduino IDE interface.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — Ultrasonic Ranging sketch + Serial Monitor page
shared.alt: Official manual page showing the Ultrasonic Ranging sketch and Serial Monitor output.

shared.origin: Freenove Super Starter Kit for ESP32-S3 — ESP32S3_Pinout.png
shared.alt: Official ESP32-S3 pinout diagram labelling every GPIO, power, and USB pin on the board.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — LED component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 1 LED, page 40)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a red LED, its longer positive leg and shorter negative leg labelled.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — resistor component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 1 LED, page 40)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a resistor with coloured value bands.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — push button component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 2 Button & LED, page 48)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a four-pin push button switch with its pin pairs labelled 1 and 2.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — LED bar graph component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 3 LED Bar, page 55)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a ten-segment LED bar graph with its two rows of pins numbered 1-10 and 11-20.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — RGB LED component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 5 RGB LED, page 71)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a four-pin RGB LED, with its longer common pin and separate red, green, and blue legs.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — active buzzer component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 6 Buzzer, page 78)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a round active buzzer with its two pins labelled 1 and 2.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — NPN transistor (S8050) component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 6 Buzzer, page 79)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of an NPN transistor with its three legs labelled emitter, base, and collector.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — rotary potentiometer component illustration (C Tutorial, Chapter 8 AD Converter, page 95)
shared.alt: Official manual illustration of a rotary potentiometer with rotation arrows and its three pins labelled 1, 3, and 2.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — photoresistor component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 11 Photoresistor & LED, page 117)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a photoresistor with its two legs labelled 1 and 2.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — thermistor component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 12 Thermistor, page 122)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a bead thermistor with two long legs.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — joystick module photo with X/Y axes (C Tutorial, Chapter 13 Joystick, page 127)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a joystick module with X and Y axis arrows and its five pins labelled 1 to 5.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — 74HC595 chip illustration (C Tutorial, Chapter 14 74HC595 & LED Bar Graph, page 132)
shared.alt: Official manual illustration of the 74HC595 shift register chip with its sixteen pins numbered.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — 7-segment display module photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 15 74HC595 & 7-Segment Display, page 138)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a one-digit seven-segment display module with its pins numbered 10, 6, 1, and 5 at the corners.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — DC motor component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 16 Relay & Motor, page 145)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a small DC motor with two terminals and a metal shaft.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — L293D motor driver chip photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 16 Relay & Motor, page 145)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of the L293D motor driver chip, a sixteen-pin integrated circuit.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — servo photo with wire labels (C Tutorial, Chapter 17 Servo, page 153)
shared.alt: Official manual illustration of a servo with its three wires labelled 1 signal, 2 power, and 3 ground.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — I2C LCD1602 module photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 18 LCD1602, page 163)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of the I2C LCD1602 module, a sixteen-by-two character display with a four-pin I2C backpack.

6.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
6.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the LED bar: ten LED-bar segments each driven from a GPIO pin through its own 220-ohm resistor to ground, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

7.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; the same single-LED circuit as Blink)
7.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the breathing light: a single LED on GPIO 2 through a 220-ohm resistor to ground, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

8.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; the meteor light reuses the Chapter 3 LED-bar circuit, page 56 / page 66)
8.alt: Official Freenove LED-bar circuit: LED-bar segments each driven from a GPIO pin through a 220-ohm resistor to ground, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

9.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
9.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the RGB LED: the red, green, and blue legs each driven from a GPIO pin through a 220-ohm resistor, with the common pin to 3.3V, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

10.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
10.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the buzzer: an active buzzer driven from GPIO 14 through an NPN transistor and a 1k-ohm resistor, and a push button on GPIO 21 held by 10k-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

4.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
4.alt: Official Freenove circuit for Button & LED: an LED on GPIO 2 through a 220-ohm resistor to ground, and a push button on GPIO 13 with 10k-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

5.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; the table lamp reuses Project 2.1's circuit)
5.alt: Official Freenove circuit for Button & LED: an LED on GPIO 2 through a 220-ohm resistor to ground, and a push button on GPIO 13 with 10k-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

13.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
13.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the potentiometer: its two outer pins to 3.3V and ground and its middle pin to GPIO 1, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

14.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; Project 9.2 Touch Lamp)
14.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the touch lamp: a jumper wire on GPIO 14 as the touch pad and an LED on GPIO 21 through a 220-ohm resistor, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

15.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
15.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the soft light: a potentiometer feeding GPIO 1 and an LED on GPIO 14 through a 220-ohm resistor, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

16.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; Project 10.2 Soft Colorful Light)
16.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the soft colourful light: three potentiometers feeding GPIO 12, 13, and 14 and an RGB LED on GPIO 38, 39, and 40 through 220-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

17.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
17.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the night lamp: a photoresistor and 10k-ohm resistor divider feeding GPIO 1 and an LED on GPIO 14 through a 220-ohm resistor, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

18.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
18.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the thermometer: a thermistor and 10k-ohm resistor divider feeding GPIO 1, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

19.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
19.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the joystick: VRX to GPIO 14, VRY to GPIO 13, SW to GPIO 12, +5V to 3.3V, and GND to ground, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

20.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
20.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the 74HC595 flowing light: the shift register driven from GPIO 12, 13, and 14 and its eight outputs driving the LED bar through 220-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

21.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
21.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the seven-segment display: the 74HC595 driven from GPIO 12, 13, and 14 with its outputs driving the display's segments through 220-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

22.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF, including Freenove's 9V-battery note)
22.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the motor: an L293D driver on GPIO 12, 13, and 14, a potentiometer on GPIO 1, the motor on the driver's outputs, and a 9V battery powering the extension board, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

23.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
23.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the servo sweep: the servo's signal wire on GPIO 21, power on 5V, and ground to GND, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

24.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; Project 17.2 Servo Knop)
24.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the servo knob: a potentiometer feeding GPIO 14 and the servo's signal wire on GPIO 21, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

25.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
25.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the LCD1602: the module's SDA on GPIO 14, SCL on GPIO 13, power on 5V, and ground to GND, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

28.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; Project 20.2 Bluetooth Control LED)
28.alt: Official Freenove circuit for the Bluetooth LED: an LED on GPIO 2 through a 220-ohm resistor to ground, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

26.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
26.alt: Official Freenove diagram showing HC-SR04 VCC connected to 5V, Trig to GPIO 13, Echo to GPIO 14, and GND to ground on the ESP32-S3 GPIO extension board.
